name: Dbrown
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the dbrown cgen instead.

name: Dbufrd
doc: Read values from a buffer on demand, using phase (index) value that is also pulled on demand. All inputs can be either demand ugen or any other ugen.

argDocs.loop: when phase exceeds number of frames in buffer, loops when set to 1

name: Dbufwr
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the dbufwr cgen instead.

name: Demand
doc: On every trigger it demands the next value from each of the demand ugens passed as args. Used to pull values from the other demand rate ugens. By design, a reset trigger only resets the demand ugens; it does not reset the value at Demand's output. Demand continues to hold its value until the next value is demanded, at which point its output value will be the first expected item in the list.

argDocs.trig: Can be any signal. A trigger happens when the signal changes from non-positive to positive.
argDocs.reset: Resets the list of ugens when triggered.

name: DemandEnvGen
doc: Plays back break point envelope contours (levels, times, shapes) given by demand ugens. The next values are called when the next node is reached.

argDocs.level: demand ugen (or other ugen) returning level values
argDocs.dur: demand ugen (or other ugen) returning time values
argDocs.shape: demand ugen (or other ugen) returning shape number - the number given is the shape number
argDocs.curve: demand ugen (or other ugen) returning curve values - if shape is 5, this is the curve factor. The possible values are: 0 - flat segments, 1 - linear segments, the default, 2 - natural exponential growth and decay. In this case, the levels must all be nonzero and the have the same sign, 3 - sinusoidal S shaped segments, 4 - sinusoidal segments shaped like the sides of a Welch window, a Float - a curvature value for all segments, an Array of Floats - curvature values for each segments.
argDocs.gate: control rate gate if gate is x >= 1, the ugen runs, if gate is 0 > x > 1, the ugen is released at the next level (doneAction), if gate is x <= 0, the ugen is sampled end held
argDocs.reset: if reset crosses from nonpositive to positive, the ugen is reset at the next level. If it is > 1, it is reset immediately.
argDocs.levelScale: demand ugen returning level scaling values
argDocs.levelBias: demand ugen returning level offset values
argDocs.timeScale: demand ugen returning time scaling values

name: Dgeom
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the dgeom cgen instead.

name: Dibrown
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the dibrown cgen instead.

name: Diwhite
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the diwhite cgen instead.

name: Dpoll
doc: Print the value of an input demand ugen. The print-out is in the form: label: value block offset: offset. WARNING: Printing values from the Server is intensive for the CPU. Poll should be used for debugging purposes.

argDocs.in: demand ugen to poll values from
argDocs.trigId: if greater than 0, a '/tr' message is sent back to the client (similar to send-trig)
argDocs.label: a label string
argDocs.run: activation switch 0 or 1 (can be a demand ugen)

name: Drand
doc: Demand rate random sequence generator. Generate a random ordering of an input sequence.

argDocs.list: array of values or other ugens

name: Dseq
doc: Demand rate sequence generator. Outputs a sequence of values, possibly repeating multiple times. Use INF as a repeat val to create an endless loop.

argDocs.list: array of values or other ugens

name: Dser
doc: Demand rate sequence generator. Generates a sequence of values like dseq, except outputs only count total values, rather than repeating.

argDocs.list: array of values or other ugens

name: Dseries
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the dseries cgen instead.

name: Dshuf
doc: Demand rate random sequence generator. Shuffle a sequence once and then output it one or more times.

argDocs.list: array of values or other ugens

name: Dstutter
doc: Replicates input values n times on demand. Both inputs can be demand rate ugens.

argDocs.numRepeats: number of repeats (can be a demand ugen)

name: Dswitch
doc: A demand rate switch. In difference to Dswitch1, Dswitch embeds all items of an input demand ugen first before looking up the next index.

argDocs.list: array of values or other ugens
argDocs.index: which of the inputs to return

name: Dswitch1
doc: A demand rate switch that can be used to select one of multiple demand rate inputs.

argDocs.list: array of values or other ugens
argDocs.index: which of the inputs to return

name: Duty
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the duty cgen instead.

name: Dwhite
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the dwhite cgen instead.

name: Dxrand
doc: Demand rate random sequence generator. Generate a random ordering of the given sequence without repeating any element until all elements have been returned.

argDocs.list: array of values or other ugens

name: TDuty
doc: This ugen has been internalised for scserver compatibility. Please use the tduty cgen instead.
